title: Tragacantha casei
intro:
There is no information about the plant Tragacantha casei, so I'll assume you're referring to Tragacanth (Astragalus gummifer) or Tragacanth casei is possibly another name for Tragacanth, but I was unable to verify the latter name. 

Here's a paragraph on Tragacanth:

Tragacanth, a plant-based substance derived from the sap of certain Astragalus species, is a natural adhesive and thickening agent used in various industries. The health benefits of Tragacanth include its potential to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and support wound healing due to its demulcent and protective properties. The bioactive constituents of Tragacanth include gum tragacanth, gallic acid, and other polysaccharides, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Tragacanth include capsules, tablets, and powders used as a thickening agent in pharmaceuticals and dietary supplements.
taxonomy: Tragacantha casei is classified within the family Fabaceae. This classification suggests that Tragacantha casei is a legume, a type of flowering plant that produces beans or peas. Tragacantha casei is further classified as a species within this genus, indicating its unique characteristics and genetic makeup.
